statements.statement: for a ∈ {0,1}, E[Y(a) | X] = E[Y | A=a, X] almost surely
statements.route: consistency rewrites Y as Y(a) on {A=a}; ignorability swaps the conditioning on A; positivity makes the conditional event non-null
statements.proof_tex: Fix a. On {A=a}, consistency gives Y = Y(a). By ignorability (Y0,Y1) ⊥ A | X, so E[Y(a) | A=a, X] = E[Y(a) | X]; positivity ensures P(A=a | X) > 0 a.s. so the conditional expectation is well-defined. Combining, E[Y | A=a, X] = E[Y(a) | A=a, X] = E[Y(a) | X].

statements.route: apply lem:cond-mean-identified to each arm and average over X (g-formula / backdoor adjustment)
statements.proof_tex: By lem:cond-mean-identified, E[Y(a) | X] = E[Y | A=a, X] a.s. for a ∈ {0,1}. Taking the difference and the outer expectation over X, E[Y(1) − Y(0)] = E_X[ E[Y | A=1, X] − E[Y | A=0, X] ] = ψ(P).

statements.statement: when A ⊥ X (randomized treatment), ψ(P) reduces to E[Y | A=1] − E[Y | A=0]
statements.route: under A ⊥ X the inner conditional means do not depend on X and the outer average collapses
statements.proof_tex: If A ⊥ X then E[Y | A=a, X] = E[Y | A=a], so the outer X-average in ψ(P) is constant in X and equals E[Y | A=1] − E[Y | A=0]. This recovers the unadjusted difference-in-means under randomization.

bibliography.citation: Imbens & Rubin (2015), Causal Inference for Statistics, Social, and Biomedical Sciences.
Rosenbaum & Rubin (1983), The central role of the propensity score, Biometrika.
Pearl (1995), Causal diagrams for empirical research, Biometrika.
